g.data-narration: Welcome to Exception Types in Java. We'll explore the complete exception hierarchy and learn to handle errors effectively.
g.text: Understanding Error Handling in Java Programming

g.data-narration: Exceptions are events that disrupt program flow. They occur during execution and must be handled to prevent program crashes.
g.text: Exceptions are events that occur during program execution, disrupting the normal flow.
They can be caught and handled to prevent program termination.

g.data-narration: The Throwable class is the root. It branches into Error for JVM problems and Exception for program exceptions.
g.text: Throwable Hierarchy - Root of All Exceptions

g.data-narration: Error represents serious JVM problems like OutOfMemoryError and StackOverflowError. These should generally not be caught.
g.text: Serious problems that applications should not catch. They indicate JVM issues.

g.data-narration: StackOverflowError happens with infinite recursion when the call stack exceeds its limit.
The Exception class is the base for all program exceptions. It has two main types: checked and unchecked.
g.text: Base class for all exceptions that programs can handle.

g.data-narration: Checked exceptions are enforced by the compiler. You must handle them with try-catch or declare them with throws.
g.text: Enforced by compiler. Must be handled (try-catch) or declared (throws).

g.data-narration: Here's how to handle checked exceptions with try-catch blocks. The compiler ensures these are properly handled.
Unchecked exceptions (RuntimeException) are not checked by the compiler. They're usually programming bugs.
g.text: NOT enforced by compiler. Usually programming bugs that should be fixed.

g.data-narration: NullPointerException occurs when you try to use a null reference. It's a common programming bug to fix.
g.text: Occurs when accessing methods/fields on a null reference:

g.data-narration: ArithmeticException happens on invalid arithmetic like division by zero. It's a bug to fix, not handle.
ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException occurs when accessing an invalid array index. Always check array bounds.
RuntimeException has many subclasses. ClassCastException and NumberFormatException are also unchecked.
Compare checked vs unchecked: compiler enforcement and handling requirements differ. Key for exam success.
Remember: Throwable is root, Error is unchecked, Exception has checked and unchecked types. Checked must be handled.
For exams, focus on the hierarchy and know which exceptions are checked versus unchecked. Practice identifying exception types in code.
g.text: Focus on the hierarchy and difference between checked and unchecked exceptions. Identify common exceptions in code.
